claimText: The International Big Cat Alliance (IBCA) was launched by India in 2023 to commemorate 50 years of Project Tiger.
evidenceExcerpt: Launched by India in 2023 to commemorate 50 years of Project Tiger
examUse: Statement-based question on the origin and founding purpose of the IBCA.
distractorHooks: Launched to mark 50 years of the Wildlife Protection Act
Launched in 2010 during the St. Petersburg Tiger Summit

claimText: The IBCA is a treaty-based inter-governmental organization.
evidenceExcerpt: the IBCA is a treaty-based inter-governmental organization.
examUse: Statement-based question on the legal and organizational nature of the IBCA.
distractorHooks: It is a specialized agency of the United Nations
It is an informal coalition without a treaty

claimText: The IBCA aims to conserve seven big cats: Tiger, Lion, Leopard, Snow Leopard, Cheetah, Jaguar, and Puma.
evidenceExcerpt: It aims to conserve 7 big cats: Tiger, Lion, Leopard, Snow Leopard, Cheetah, Jaguar, and Puma.
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question identifying the specific species covered by the alliance.
distractorHooks: Covers only the five big cats found in India

claimText: Among the seven big cats conserved by the IBCA, Puma and Jaguar are the only ones not found in India.
evidenceExcerpt: All except Puma and Jaguar are found in India
examUse: Statement-based question on the geographical distribution of the IBCA big cats relative to India.
distractorHooks: Cheetah is not found in India
Jaguar is naturally found in the Indian subcontinent
Snow Leopard is not found in India

claimText: The five IBCA big cats found in India are protected under Schedule I of the Wildlife Protection Act (WPA), 1972.
evidenceExcerpt: All except Puma and Jaguar are found in India and protected under Schedule I of WPA, 1972.
examUse: Statement-based question on the legal protection status of big cats in India.
distractorHooks: Protected under Schedule II of WPA, 1972
Only the Tiger and Lion are in Schedule I

claimText: The apex decision-making body of the IBCA is the IBCA Assembly, which meets every two years.
evidenceExcerpt: its apex decision-making body is the IBCA Assembly, which meets every 2 years.
examUse: Statement-based question on the governance structure and meeting frequency of the IBCA.
distractorHooks: The apex body is the IBCA Secretariat
